# repometrics (Full Agent Reference)

## What It Does

`repometrics` analyzes a local Python repository and outputs deterministic:
- category metrics
- category scores
- final score
- health status for automation

No network calls. Read-only analysis.

## Install

```bash
pip install repometrics
```

## Commands

Primary command:

```bash
repometrics check
```

Compatibility alias:

```bash
repometrics scan
```

## Flags

```bash
repometrics check \
  [--path DIR] \
  [--days N] \
  [--json] \
  [--min-score N] \
  [--confirm-test-matches] \
  [--no-color]
```

- `--path`: target directory (default `.`)
- `--days`: git window in days (default `30`)
- `--json`: emit machine-readable JSON
- `--min-score`: health threshold (default `70`, range `0..100`)
- `--confirm-test-matches`: interactive confirmation for ambiguous non-`test_*` test matches
- `--no-color`: disable ANSI colors in text mode

## Exit Codes

- `0`: healthy (`final_score >= min_score`)
- `1`: unhealthy (`final_score < min_score`)
- `2`: runtime/validation error

## JSON Output Format

Top-level keys:
- `schema_version`
- `path`
- `days`
- `generated_at`
- `categories`
- `scoring`
- `healthy`
- `min_score`
- `exit_code`
- `warnings`
- `errors`

`categories` contains:
- `structure`
- `dependencies`
- `git`
- `hygiene`

Each category has:
- `status` (`ok`, `partial`, `unavailable`)
- `metrics`
- `warnings`
- `errors`

`scoring` contains:
- `category_scores`
- `weights_used`
- `final_score`

## Minimal JSON Example

```bash
repometrics check --json --min-score 75
```

## Text Output Notes

- Includes final score summary and threshold/health
- Includes per-category metric sections
- Includes colored score bar (red/yellow/green bands)
- Color auto-disables in non-TTY; use `--no-color` to force plain output

## Common Agent Workflows

Health gate in CI/automation:

```bash
repometrics check --json --min-score 80
```

Parse result:
- use process exit code for pass/fail branch logic
- use JSON fields (`healthy`, `scoring.final_score`, `categories.*`) for diagnostics

Backwards compatibility:
- agents may call `repometrics scan`, but `check` is canonical
